input.draft: Urban farms now produce approximately 15 percent of the world's food supply, according to the United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization. In Detroit alone, more than 1,400 community gardens and farms operate on previously vacant lots. Professor Maria Santos, who directs the Center for Urban Agriculture at Greenfield University, has studied the phenomenon since 2015. 'We are seeing a fundamental shift in how cities think about food production,' she says. The average urban farm generates $50,000 in annual revenue per acre, compared to $2,000 per acre for conventional rural farming, according to a 2024 study published in the Journal of Sustainable Agriculture.
The library saw a 22 percent increase in membership last year.

Europe's night train network has expanded by 40 percent since 2020, with Austrian operator ÖBB Nightjet leading the revival. The Vienna-to-Paris route, relaunched in December 2021, now carries 200,000 passengers annually. A single night train journey from Amsterdam to Barcelona produces 90 percent fewer carbon emissions than the equivalent flight, according to the European Environment Agency. Night trains now serve 28 routes across 15 countries. EU Transport Commissioner Henrik Johansson has proposed €8 billion in infrastructure investment to support further expansion by 2030.
